review_comment: I understand that this fixes problem with NULL pointer exception but unfortunately now systems continue write to system that is no longer valid.
In my testing the i/o errors out and returns an error code, which is a huge improvement over the NULL pointer exception.
Remove the "Fixes https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/zephyr/issues/73501" from commit and description as this does not fix the problem, it only makes it manifest in a less violent way.
But it does fix "the problem", which is NULL pointer dereference caused by a user removing a usb drive during file i/o.
